scenario: Create Prompt API Validation Test Suite

testCases.name: Create prompt with valid payload - BR to TS

request.promptText: Understand (a) project overview and (b) specific business requirement with respect to a Dynamics 365 implementation.Read the business requirements, business logic and flow diagrams. Keeping the key success factor to be functional characteristics - completeness, correctness, appropriateness, non-ambiguousness - for each of the feature or functional requirement perform a comprehensive requirements analysis, then generate a comprehensive set of test scenarios based on the provided user story/ features for software testing. Each scenario should consider functional characteristics - completeness, correctness, appropriateness, non-ambiguousness of the application and then generate positive and negative test scenarios to cover a wide range of test scenarios as well as consider leveraging equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, decision table or cause effect table, use case testing for test case design. Provide the result in json array format. The json should contain  test scenario description (key name: description), an unique test scenario ID (key name: id) and the priority of the test scenario (key name: priority) (values: one of high, low or medium)
request.promptDescription: Business Requirement to Test Scenario prompt

testCases.name: Create prompt with valid payload - TS to TC

request.promptText: Context: User is on the login page. Given the test scenario description and relevant images, you need to create test cases covering the entire end-to-end scenario, including any necessary preconditions, setup, and post-conditions. Focus on end-to-end scenarios only. Use relevant images only (ignore irrelevant ones). Strict Instructions: Relevance: Use only relevant images related to that Test scenario. When information added in 'Additional information' section overrides the 'Project Overview' data, priority should be given for 'Additional information' section. Comprehensive Coverage: Cover all preconditions, setup, and post-conditions. Logical Flow: Ensure the test cases have logical start and end points. Exit Criteria: Clearly define what constitutes successful completion of the test scenario. Well-Formed JSON: Ensure the JSON response is well-formed and does not exceed the maximum token limit. Test Data Format: All testData must be provided as a key-value pair object. Strict Adherence: Strictly follow all instructions provided in this prompt. End Point Exit Criteria: The LLM should automatically define the exit criteria based on the scenario description, focusing on key actions/states that should be achieved. For example: 'The user should be able to successfully [action], which will result in [state]. The user should be able to [perform the action] and [verify the state].' Output Format: The test cases should be output in JSON array format with the following structure: 1. tcSeqNo: Test case sequence number (must-have). 2. tcDescription: Test case description (must-have). 3. testData: Test data (All testData must be provided as a key-value pair object). 4. tcPriority: Priority of the test case (high, medium, or low - must-have). 5. tcPrecondition: Test case precondition (must-have). 6. smoke: Applicable for smoke testing (0 or 1 - must-have). 7. regression: Applicable for regression testing (0 or 1 - must-have). 8. uat: Applicable for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) (0 or 1 - must-have). 9. stepArray: Array containing steps and expected results: - tcStep: Test case step (must-have). - tcResult: Expected result of the step (must-have).
request.promptDescription: Test Scenario to Test Case prompt
